notes: Singleplayer is working fine after initial stutters. Alt + tabbing crashes the game as in the previous one.
title: Farming Simulator 19

notes: The game starts and runs fine. Played through the tutorials no problem. Only glitch I have is some flickering shadows.
title: Farming Simulator 19

notes:
High settings, performance is great with really really rare shutters (I don't even count them). The bad thing is texture glitches, specifically shadows flick every 3-4 seconds. The plow texture are changing from high to low resolution and vise versa from time to time. Very very hard foliage mainly grass/crops flickering around me, but after maybe 50 meters from me. Tho it's playable I forget about it since it's not close to me ever, only in the distance.
I tried every possible settings combination and render distance but it's all the same. It's rendering issue with lights since I don't see many flickering in night.
title: Farming Simulator 19

notes:
Singleplayer is working fine but with some flickering shadows that seems to be due to game itself.
Alt + tabbing crashes the game. Setting msaa > 0 also crash the game (default in High settings).
Impossible to use recommended nvidia drivers (nvidia 396) since game starts but is full black. Only menus are visibles, no 3D.

title: Farming Simulator 19

notes: It run fine with some shadow and texture grass/crop flickering, but after I got my drivers updated from 410.78 to 415.18 every flickering and texture glitches are gone. Apparently there was some bug when rendering Vulkan games which was fixed (nvidia site changelogs). So this needs minor tweak, updating driver. Fps is 60 constant with high-very high settings. Also ALT-TAB works if you chose to play game in windowed mode which on my system is fullscreen like. I enjoy it very much.
title: Farming Simulator 19

notes: The game runs great with good performance. Alt-Tab crashes the game. Multiplayer connection works after renaming ~/.steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/amd64/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgnutls.so.26 to libgnutls.so.26.bak. Renaming this file may prevent other games from connecting to the internet. Steam client update may reset this change.
title: Farming Simulator 19

notes:
Runs perfectly now with Geforce GTX 1060 and nvidia-drivers-415 and default D3D_11 rendered in game.xml. No more flickering shadows in the fields.
Only Alt-Tab is KO when setting Fullscreen.
title: Farming Simulator 19

notes: Performance and  Singleplayer are fine. No tabbing out to Desktop without Crash. After making a copy of  ~/.steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/amd64/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgnutls.so.26 and rename it to libgnutls.so.26.bak. Multiplayer works fine on 2 Different Systems (Q9400 with rx 460 4gig) and this one. Thanks to the Guy with this tweak
title: Farming Simulator 19

notes: 415 drivers solved the flickering bushes/trees, haven't tested multiplayer.
title: Farming Simulator 19

notes: Had to install gnutls28 (Version 3.3.19) compatibility package via pacman. Using the most recent gnutls 3.5.19 immediately crashes the game when trying to connect to the master server in multiplayer. Since I discourage everyone (including myself) deleting or renaming files within your Steam installation, I use the following launch parameter to the game in my Steam library: LD_PRELOAD=/usr/lib/libgnutls.so.28 %command% . NOTE: With LD_PRELOAD, only the particular title is advised to use the libgnutls.so other than the Steam-supplied version. Other games in your library are not affected by this change, gaining internet access just as normal. No more messing with your Steam/Proton installation. Since I don't have any other derivates running, I can't say for sure what the working libgnutls.so library filename is called on _YOUR_ system. You have to check this for yourself (hint: /usr/lib/libgnutls.so*).
title: Farming Simulator 19
